hypothesis: If LoQI, a stereochemistry-aware diffusion model augmented with E/Z and R/S topology encodings and trained on AIMNet2-optimized ChEMBL3D conformers, predicts atomic coordinates for new molecules, then the generated conformers will be low in energy near the dataset minimum, preserve topology, accurately reproduce stereochemical configurations (R/S and E/Z), and closely match reference structures (low RMSD), with some cases yielding lower-energy conformers than the dataset as confirmed by DFT validation.
